zh.lifeSummary: Agnes Randall是John Muther Randall, Mayor of Bergen的女兒，於1521年出生在英國瓦維其可爾考文垂。她於1547年2月3日與Reverend John Foxe在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾哈爾可特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1605年4月22日在英國倫敦享年84歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦 聖 基路士 維特 可利普萊加特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Agnes Randall是John Muther Randall, Mayor of Bergen的女儿，于1521年出生在英国瓦維其可爾考文垂。她于1547年2月3日与Reverend John Foxe在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾哈爾可特结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1605年4月22日在英国倫敦享年84岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可倫敦 聖 基路士 維特 可利普萊加特。

nameMeanings.name: Randall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): common colloquial pronunciation of   Randolph  .

variants.name: Randell
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Americanized form of German   Randel  .

variants.name: Randon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel.
English: probably a variant of Randall , with assimilation of n-l to n-n.
French (northern): from the ancient Germanic personal name Rando, a short form of names based on the element rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’.

variants.name: Randol
meanings: English: altered form of   Randall  .

variants.name: Randal
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Randal in Gulen, a compound of Old Norse runnr ‘copse, thicket, grove’ + dalr ‘valley’.

variants.name: Randles
meanings: English and Irish (Louth): variant of Randall , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland this is sometimes a variant of Reynolds .

variants.name: Randle
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Americanized form of German Randel or of its Bavarian variant Randl.

variants.name: Rendell
meanings: English (Somerset, Devon, and Dorset): variant of   Randall  .

variants.name: Randel
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
South German: variant of   Rand   3.

variants.name: Randolph
meanings: English and German: from Randolf, an ancient Germanic personal name composed of the elements rand ‘rim (of a shield), shield’ + wolf ‘wolf’. This was introduced into England by the Normans in Old French forms of two different ancient Germanic personal names which became confused with each other: Randulf (from rand ‘(shield-)edge’ + wulf ‘wolf’) and Rannulf (from hraf(a)n ‘raven’ + wulf ‘wolf’).

History: An American family bearing this surname are descended from William Randolph (c. 1651–1711), a planter and merchant, a member of a family that originally came from Sussex, England. William Randolph emigrated from Warwickshire to VA c. 1673. He was a forebear of Thomas Jefferson and Robert E. Lee. Randolph had seven sons, each of whom inherited an estate, the name of which was sometimes added to their own, such as Sir John Randolph of Tazewell. His great-grandsons included Edmund Randolph (1753–1813), first attorney general of the US and one of the framers of the US Constitution, and the diplomat and statesman John Randolph of Roanoke (1773–1833), who served as US minister to Russia.

variants.name: Rendall
meanings: English: variant of   Randall  .
Scottish: habitational name from Rendall (Orkney), named with a stream-name of uncertain origin + Old Norse dalr ‘valley’.
In some cases possibly also an Americanized form of Swedish   Rendahl  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Randall was born in 1521, in Coventry, Warwickshire, England as the daughter of John Muther Randall, Mayor of Bergen. She married Reverend John Foxe on 3 February 1547, in Charlecote, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 22 April 1605, in London, England, at the age of 84, and was buried in London St Giles without Cripplegate, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnes Randall est née en 1521, à Coventry, Warwickshire, Angleterre, fille de John Muther Randall, Mayor of Bergen. Elle a épousé Reverend John Foxe le 3 février 1547, à Charlecote, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 22 avril 1605, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterrée à London St Giles without Cripplegate, Middlesex, Angleterre.

it.lifeSummary: Agnes Randall è nata il nel 1521 nel Coventry, Warwickshire, England come figlia di John Muther Randall, Mayor of Bergen. Lei sposato Reverend John Foxe il 3 febbraio 1547, nel Charlecote, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 22 aprile 1605, nel London, England, all'età di 84 anni, e fu sepolta a London St Giles without Cripplegate, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Randallは1521年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアコベントリーでJohn Muther Randall, Mayor of Bergenの娘として生まれた。1547年2月3日にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアチャーレコトでReverend John Foxeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1605年4月22日にイギリスロンドンで84歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドン スト ギレス ウィソウト クリップレガトにて埋葬された。

ko.St Nicholas Acons, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 에드먼드 테 킨그 위스 스트 닉홀아스 악스
ko.St Botolph Aldgate, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 세인트 벗프'스 c후르치 알드가트
ko.Saint Giles without Cripplegate, London,: 영국 미들레세스 런던 스트 자일스 위토우트 크리플레가트
ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Randall(은)는 1521년 영국 워윅실르 커븐트리에서 John Muther Randall, Mayor of Bergen의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1547년 2월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 찰렉트에서 Reverend John Foxe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1605년 4월 22일 영국 런던에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던 스트 자일스 위토우트 크리플레가트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Agnes Randall nasceu em 1521, em Coventry, Warwickshire, Inglaterra filha de John Muther Randall, Mayor of Bergen. Ela casou-se com Reverend John Foxe em 3 de fevereiro de 1547, em Charlecote, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 22 de abril de 1605, em London, Inglaterra, com 84 anos, e foi sepultada em London St Giles without Cripplegate, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Agnes Randall родилась в 1521 г. в местности ковентри, варвиккшир, Англия. Её отца звали John Muther Randall, Mayor of Bergen. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Reverend John Foxe 3 февраля 1547 г. в местности чарлекот, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 22 апреля 1605 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 84 лет и была похоронена в местности лондон ст джилесь витоут крипплегэит, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Agnes Randall nació en 1521, en Coventry, Warwickshire, Inglaterra como hija de John Muther Randall, Mayor of Bergen. Se casó con Reverend John Foxe el 3 de febrero de 1547, en Charlecote, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 22 de abril de 1605, en London, Inglaterra, a la edad de 84 años, y fue sepultada en London St Giles without Cripplegate, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
